behavior_summary: U8P_AXE2::look is the object's U8P_AXE2::look/inspect handler. When the player inspects this item it displays a single short text/bark: "axe ".
After emitting the text the process suspends/pauses (inferred from the presence of a suspend node), waiting for the player to dismiss the message.
There is no branching, menu interaction, external calls, or global flag access — this is a minimal static description response.
Item property metadata (damage values and types) exists for the object but is not referenced by the U8P_AXE2::look process.
notable_control_flow: Single-entry process invoked by the engine's U8P_AXE2::look/inspect action (process type 0x0208).
Emit one player-facing Bark/DialogueLine then suspend the process.
No conditional branches, no reads/writes of global flags, and no function calls.
open_questions: Why the displayed text includes a trailing space ('axe ')? (Could be formatting or data artifact.)
Why the item's damage/type properties are present in metadata but unused by the U8P_AXE2::look handler — are they used elsewhere?
What exact semantics does the Unknown 'suspend' node have in this script instance (inferred as standard Titan suspend)?

main_function.name: U8P_AXE2::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description shown to the player when they inspect this item.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the player's Look/inspect action on the object (engine invokes U8P_AXE2::look processType 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_AXE2::look
function_map.role: Look/inspect handler that displays the object's short name/description.
